content:
# Completion Vote
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Vote: Complete

## Evidence

- `InvocationMetadata` already carries `backend_used` and `model_used` (`src/contexts/agent_execution/model.rs:248-256`).
- Primary stage persistence builds `RecordProducer::Agent` from `envelope.metadata.backend_used.family` and `envelope.metadata.model_used.model_id`, then writes that producer onto both payload and artifact records (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4647-4655`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4693-4712`).
- Supporting artifact paths also use envelope metadata before persistence: completion (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:138-170`), prompt review (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:111-139`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:170-186`), and final review (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:239-265`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:351-380`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:443-474`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:559-584`).
- `ArtifactRecord` persists producer metadata, and the filesystem adapter serializes the full artifact record directly to `history/artifacts/*.json` with `serde_json::to_string_pretty(artifact)` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/model.rs:328-344`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:1091-1106`).
- An existing regression test asserts persisted artifacts use envelope metadata rather than configured targets (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1513-1605`).

## Remaining Work

None.
